bids.reasoning: This is the opening at t=0.0 with no opponent offers or messages to infer their preferences, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. With no time pressure yet, I should anchor strongly on my best values across all issues.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, and the opponent’s only offer is the minimum-utility bid for me on every issue, so there is no evidence of meaningful concession yet. I am targeting a very high utility while avoiding an exact repeat of my opening bid, conceding only on my lowest-weight issueA and holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.04 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the all-valueA offer. I will make only a small concession on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and D.

I am still targeting a very high utility bid because it is very early in the negotiation (t=0.06), and the opponent has not yet shown any concession, repeatedly offering the lowest-value package for me. I will continue making small concessions only on my lowest-weight issue, issueA, while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and especially D.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.935 by conceding only on my lowest-weight issue while preserving issueB, issueC, and issueD at my top value. Since it is still very early at t=0.08, there is no need to make large concessions, especially because the opponent has not conceded at all and keeps repeating the all-valueA bid. No opponent messages were provided, so I am relying only on the offer pattern.

I am still targeting a high utility bid because it is very early in the negotiation (t=0.10), and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeatedly offering the minimum values for every issue. I will finish conceding on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the much more important issues B, C, and especially D.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.89 because it is still very early at t=0.12, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has held firm at all valueA offers with no concession and no messages, so I will continue conceding only on my lower-weight issues while protecting issueC and issueD.

I targeted a still-high utility bid (about 0.856) by conceding further on the lower-weight issueB while protecting the more important issueC and issueD. It is still early at t=0.14, and the opponent has shown no concession at all, repeatedly offering all valueA, so I will continue gradual concessions rather than making a large drop.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.824 by continuing only small concessions on lower-weight issues while preserving issueC and issueD, which matter most to me. The opponent has held firm at all valueA with no concession, but at t=0.16 it is still early, so I should not collapse toward their position yet; I will make a modest non-repeating concession from my last bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.79 by conceding further only on the lower-weight issueB while preserving high-weight issueC and issueD at valueF. The opponent has repeatedly offered all valueA with no concession, but it is still early at t=0.18, so I should not drop major issues yet and should avoid repeating my last exact bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.76 by conceding fully on the two lowest-weight issues while preserving my highest-weight issues C and D at valueF. The opponent has held firm at all valueA with no concession signal, and since we are only at t=0.20 I should not rush to concede on the more important issues yet, but I will avoid repeating my previous bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.712 by keeping my highest-weight issueD at valueF while conceding further on issueC. Time is still early at t=0.22, so I should not move close to the opponent’s all-valueA position yet, especially since their offers show no concession at all.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.66 by holding firm on the most important issueD while conceding one step on issueC after already conceding issueA and issueB fully. Time is still early-to-mid at t=0.24, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s all-valueA bid, especially since their offer history shows no concession at all and there are no messages adding credible new information.

I am targeting a utility around 0.62 by continuing gradual concessions on lower/medium priority issues while holding issueD, my highest-weight issue, at valueF. The opponent has repeatedly offered all valueA with no concession, and with time still only at 0.26 I should not collapse to their position, but I will avoid repeating my last bid and make a modest move on issueC.

I am targeting a utility around 0.565 by continuing to concede on issueC while preserving issueD, my highest-weight issue, at valueF. At t=0.28 there is still time left, but the opponent has held completely firm at all valueA offers and provided no messages, so I am making a measured concession without dropping my most important issue.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.52, continuing my gradual concession path while preserving issueD, my highest-weight issue, at valueF. At t=0.30 there is still time, but the opponent has held firm at all valueA with no messages or sign of concession, so I will concede the remaining lower-priority issueC before moving on issueD.

I am targeting a still-positive utility around 0.456 by keeping all already-conceded lower issues at valueA while making the first small concession on my highest-weight issueD from valueF to valueE. Time is only at 0.32, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s all-valueA demand yet, but the opponent has held completely firm on valueA across every issue, so a clear concession may test whether they respond.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.392 by conceding further only on issueD, which is my most important remaining issue but the only one not yet near the opponent’s repeated all-valueA position. Time is still only at 0.34, so I should not fully capitulate, but the opponent has shown no concession at all and consistently offers valueA on every issue.

I targeted a utility around 0.33 by conceding further only because the opponent has held completely firm at all valueA offers, which are worth just 0.20 to me. Since time is still only 0.36, I am not going all the way to their position, but I am making a valid incremental concession on my highest-weight issue to test whether they will move.

I am targeting a still-positive utility while continuing the concession path because the opponent has held completely firm at all valueA offers and has shown no concession. At t=0.38 there is some time left, but given their repeated stance, I will make a further concession from my previous bid while preserving a small gain on my most important issue, issueD.

At t=0.40 the opponent has held completely firm at all valueA offers, with no messages indicating any alternative priorities. Since we have already conceded through low-weight issues and the opponent has not reciprocated, I am targeting a minimal positive utility agreement that they have repeatedly signaled they would accept, rather than risking a breakdown by insisting on higher issueD values.
